tweets.text:
A client came to us with 0.8% cold email reply rate.

We made one change:
Replaced the generic opener with a specific trigger event.

"Congrats on your Series A — scaling outbound during hypergrowth is tricky. Here's how we helped [similar company] do it."

Reply rate: 11% in 30 days.

tweets.text:
The best B2B outbound teams share one habit:

They talk to customers weekly.

Not to sell.
To listen.

The exact words your customers use to describe their pain?

That's your cold email copy. Verbatim.

tweets.text:
Soft launching an private network for cold-email agencies, GTME's and outbound experts.

We pay a CPQM that ranges from $200-$1,000+

(Cost Per Qualified Meeting)

Offers are all sophisticated and have ACV's of $50,000+ so our buyers will be a little strict on quality.. but, if you're a savage - you will print.

If this is interesting, RT and like so it gets exposure - I will likely scrap this idea if there's no interest.

Already have a few offers that you could jump on.

--

What this looks like for you:

1. Log into our marketplace
2. Look for offers you want to run
3. Review the offer, CPQM and briefing
4. Accept the offer
5. Get your mailboxes, lists & copy set up
6. Run your campaigns
7. Forward Positive Replies to offer owner
8. Your PR is then evaluated for 48 hours
9. You will get a milestone or decline notification

Your job is to simply print PR's and get paid for them.

This is high risk for retainer-brained operators who suck, this is low risk and open season for the dogs.

Our moat is our buyers, none of you could mimic this on the buy side.

Offers are mainly financial services/products, consulting, advisory, all B2B in adjacent sectors.

tweets.text:
Cold email + WhatsApp works best when the text adds value.

Message 1:

"Hey Mark"

Message 2:

"Noticed your site has no outbound lead capture but competitors are running cold email heavily. Curious if you're testing outbound right now?"

That's how conversations start.

author.name: Jesse Samuel
author.bio: Founder @satellyteai | helping founders turn buying signals into booked meetings | deploying AI in the physical world

tweets.text:
i've watched people lose their entire business email because they sent cold emails from their primary domain.

if that domain gets flagged, you lose more than outbound campaigns.

you lose ENTIRE business communications:

→ client emails gone
→ invoices not landing
→ partner emails bouncing
→ everything tied to that domain.. destroyed

it takes 3-6 months to rebuild that trust once it's gone.

separate domains for outbound. clean .com domains from a reputable registrar. two to three inboxes per domain max.

i've seen people spin up 10 inboxes on a single domain and lose the lot when it got flagged.

6 months of pipeline.. gone over a domain choice.
